lines.text: Good evening, Madison. Quinn Foley here. The Purge is now officially underway as of {{CLOCK}}. We are {{ELAPSED}} into the twelve-hour cycle, and PRGE is live on the wire.
I'm going to be honest with you — my hands are shaking a little bit. They always shake at the start. But that's what we're here for, isn't it? To sit with you while the law takes a nap.

Weather for the evening: we're looking at 41 degrees right now, clear skies over Madison, southwest winds at 8 miles per hour. A cold front is tracking in from the northwest, expected to arrive around midnight. Visibility excellent for the first few hours.
If you're barricaded and you've got windows, you'll have a clear view of what's happening outside until roughly 23:00. After that, conditions will deteriorate. Dress warm if you have to go anywhere.

Pre-Purge sports update: the Packers are two weeks out from their divisional matchup. Local betting pools are already running despite the—well, despite everything. Also worth noting: historical data shows that Purge-night activity spikes correlate inversely with whether Wisconsin's teams won the day before.
The Packers lost yesterday. So statistically speaking, tonight might be a longer night than usual.

Station operations note: we are running full power from the primary rig. All backup systems are hot. We've got signal redundancy in place, and I want every listener to know that if you lose us, we will be back. We always come back.
So settle in, folks. Pour something hot or cold depending on your temperament. We're going to be here all night. You're not alone out there. Stay with us.

tickerItems.text: Capitol perimeter fortified and locked down as of 18:45 — official Level-10 protection in effect.
MorningCare Triage is standing by with full staffing; call-in lines open at 07:01 sharp.
Lake Mendota state boat launch closed for the duration; all craft advised to return to dock.
PurgeShield Panel installations reported 87% completion across Madison metro ahead of nightfall.
